title: robotstxtr: Engine-Aware 'robots.txt' Policy with Google's Matcher Algorithm
description: Evaluates whether URLs may be crawled according to robots.txt rules in R. A versioned engine-aware facade keeps neutral fetch evidence, per-engine status policy, robots product tokens, and matcher capabilities separate, returning explicit non-decisions when policy is undocumented or a matcher is unavailable. The available matcher is a vendored, upstream-test-validated snapshot of Google's open-source C++ robots.txt matcher, preserving Googlebot behavior. Fetches use a deterministic, conservative policy built on httr2, with fetch origins constructed by rurl.
notes: The bundled matcher derives from Google's robotstxt project (https://github.com/google/robotstxt), distributed under the Apache License 2.0; see the package NOTICE. Fetch origins are constructed with the sibling rurl package.
references: Koster, M., Illyes, G., Zeller, H., & Sassman, L. (2022). RFC 9309: Robots Exclusion Protocol. IETF.
Google LLC. Google Robots.txt Parser and Matcher Library. https://github.com/google/robotstxt
